pr.title: Split MetroTRK and Dolphin SDK code from the CW stdlib, split player.s from text_1
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that split identifiable chunks out of generic text blobs: player code was moved out of asm/text_1.s into asm/player.s, MetroTRK debugger code and Dolphin SDK code were split away from the CodeWarrior stdlib area in asm/text_2.s, and the remaining stdlib blob was renamed/continued as asm/text_3.s. ldscript.lcf was updated to preserve object ordering with player.o, text_2.o, text_3.o, MetroTRK.o, and dolphin_sdk.o in the right places. The PR body says player boundaries were clear because every function had an assert referencing player.c.

key_files.role: Original large assembly blob reduced after player code was split out.
New assembly file for player-related code split from text_1.s.
Assembly blob reorganized after MetroTRK and Dolphin SDK code were separated from the CodeWarrior stdlib region.
New/renamed remaining CodeWarrior stdlib assembly segment after splitting text_2.s.
New assembly file for combined MetroTRK debugger code, including symbols such as TRKHandleRequestEvent, TRKInitializeEventQueue, TRKInitializeNub, and InitMetroTRK.
New assembly file for combined Dolphin SDK code.
Updated object order and comments to reflect the new split files.

what_changed: Created asm/player.s and moved player code out of asm/text_1.s.
Created asm/MetroTRK.s for MetroTRK debugger code previously embedded in the mixed text_2 area.
Created asm/dolphin_sdk.s for Dolphin SDK code previously embedded in the mixed text_2 area.
Created/used asm/text_3.s for the remaining CodeWarrior stdlib assembly after text_2 was split.
Updated ldscript.lcf to add player.o(.text) and text_2.o(.text) after text_1.o(.text).
Updated ldscript.lcf to replace the old combined 'CW PPC and Dolphin SDK' text_2.o entry with separate sections for remaining CW PPC stdlib, MetroTRK, and Dolphin SDK.
The diff is dominated by relocation of assembly text between files rather than evidence of semantic code edits.
smart_moves: Used source-file assert strings as boundary evidence: the PR body specifically notes player.s boundaries were apparent because every function had an assert with player.c.
Separated known vendor/runtime components from anonymous text blobs, making future decompilation work easier to search and reason about.
Preserved linker ordering explicitly in ldscript.lcf instead of only moving files, reducing risk that object ordering or addresses would drift.
Added linker-script comments naming the separated regions: remaining CW PPC stdlib, combined MetroTRK debugger code, and combined Dolphin SDK code.
Chose file names that encode provenance or subsystem rather than leaving all code in numbered text blobs.
decomp_lessons: When a blob contains functions with repeated assert references to a source filename, those assert strings can be strong evidence for carving out a coherent assembly file.
Large asm-only PRs can still be high-value if they reduce anonymous text_N blobs into meaningful subsystem files while preserving link order.
For SDK/runtime code, splitting by external library provenance can be more useful than waiting for every function to be named.
Every assembly split that changes object boundaries should be paired with ldscript.lcf updates so future matching work starts from stable section order.
It is useful to leave remaining unknown or mixed runtime code in a numbered placeholder, such as text_3.s, when the exact subcomponents are not yet fully identified.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem/library names for identified blobs: MetroTRK.s for MetroTRK debugger code and dolphin_sdk.s for Dolphin SDK code.
Use source-derived names when assert strings identify the original C file: player.s from player.c assert evidence.
Use text_N.s names for remaining unidentified or partially classified assembly regions.
Linker objects mirror assembly filenames: player.o, text_2.o, text_3.o, MetroTRK.o, dolphin_sdk.o.
ldscript comments describe provenance with phrases like 'Remaining CW PPC stdlib', 'Combined MetroTRK debugger code', and 'Combined Dolphin SDK code'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Relocate contiguous assembly ranges into new .s files while retaining .include "macros.inc" and .section .text declarations.
Preserve function labels and existing global symbols during file moves, including known TRK-prefixed symbols and unknown func_/lbl_ names.
Use ldscript.lcf object ordering to maintain the original binary layout after splitting assembly blobs.
Use assert strings and known library symbol prefixes, such as TRK/MetroTRK, as practical split-boundary signals.
Treat huge add/delete counts cautiously in review because they may represent file movement rather than code generation changes.

review_feedback.note: No inline review comments or textual review feedback were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile functions from asm/player.s after PR #23.
Find later PRs that rename or decompile MetroTRK symbols split into asm/MetroTRK.s.
Find later PRs that further split asm/dolphin_sdk.s into specific Dolphin SDK modules.
Search for ldscript.lcf changes around text_2.o, text_3.o, MetroTRK.o, and dolphin_sdk.o to track subsequent blob refinement.
Search for use of assert strings as file-boundary evidence in other early assembly split PRs.
